human_verification.notes: Reviewed manifest, authority-source policy, scenario coverage, results, readiness review, deployment notes, and marketing notes for consistency with issue #144 and the repository delivery contract.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to commerce-platform configuration, administration, workflow governance, integration planning, release planning, and operational troubleshooting guidance.
Real public authoritative vendor sources are documented with publisher, authority reason, review date, refresh interval, decay policy, and current status in manifest.yaml.

The local package defines e-commerce-platform-specific scenarios, shared-meta-agent request and response contracts, and fallback behavior when delegated outputs are unavailable, stale, or low-confidence.
delegated_meta_agent_evidence: Best-practice corpus expansion is delegated to #225 with safe fallback to the local commerce-platform source baseline.
Scenario normalization, readiness consistency checks, deployment packaging normalization, and commercialization wording review are delegated to #302 through #305 with local-artifact fallback.
Authority-source policy normalization, boundary-routing review, and scaffolding consistency review are delegated to #306 through #308 without weakening the specialist-owned baseline.
notes: Structured evidence supports the validated claim required by issue #144.
Evaluation thresholds exceed the issue minimum with 12 scenarios and a 1.00 pass rate against a 0.92 threshold.
Deployment package documents runtime model, tenant isolation, adaptation constraints, monitoring and logging expectations, rollback path, and deferred shared-meta-agent initialization.
Marketing readiness notes document buyer profile, packaging, subscription model, proof points, and go-to-market gaps without widening the technical scope.
